title_en: Auditor General report on lab-services procurement failures and withheld records
description_en: The Auditor General of Alberta reported on community laboratory services procurement, documenting approximately 109 million dollars in losses. The report stated that the Health Minister directed Alberta Health Services to proceed after projected savings collapsed, that approximately 1,200 documents were withheld, and that records including a former chief executive's notebooks were destroyed during the audit.
verification_notes: Sourced to CBC summary (R-D0000002) and Global News (R-007, 109 million figure verified). Load-bearing primary still owed: the OAG release and full report once the office-of-the-auditor-general outlet exists. Distinct from the 2022-2023 lab-privatization events EVT-014/016/018. Lead to verify: possible NDP opposition response from David Shepherd (health critic) in Question Period circa 2025-11-24; previously carried in a contaminated EVT-019 record but never sourced. If real, source via Hansard or contemporaneous QP coverage before attaching.

update_log.change: Created from harvest batch; suppressed; OAG primary pending outlet.

Added R-007 (Global News, 109 million dollar loss confirmed) as additional source. Removed single-source standards_flag. Added verification lead for possible Shepherd opposition response recovered from a contaminated EVT-019 record (never sourced; preserved as a research lead, not propagated as a claim).
